root_text.sn56.32:1.2: ‘ahaṁ dukkhaṁ ariyasaccaṁ yathābhūtaṁ anabhisamecca, dukkhasamudayaṁ ariyasaccaṁ yathābhūtaṁ anabhisamecca, dukkhanirodhaṁ ariyasaccaṁ yathābhūtaṁ anabhisamecca, dukkhanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ ariyasaccaṁ yathābhūtaṁ anabhisamecca sammā dukkhassantaṁ karissāmī’ti—
root_text.sn56.32:2.2: ‘ahaṁ khadirapattānaṁ vā saralapattānaṁ vā āmalakapattānaṁ vā puṭaṁ karitvā udakaṁ vā tālapattaṁ vā āharissāmī’ti—
root_text.sn56.32:2.4: evameva kho, bhikkhave, yo evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.sn56.32:2.5: ‘ahaṁ dukkhaṁ ariyasaccaṁ yathābhūtaṁ anabhisamecca …pe… dukkhanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ ariyasaccaṁ yathābhūtaṁ anabhisamecca sammā dukkhassantaṁ karissāmī’ti—
root_text.sn56.32:3.1: Yo ca kho, bhikkhave, evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.sn56.32:3.2: ‘ahaṁ dukkhaṁ ariyasaccaṁ yathābhūtaṁ abhisamecca, dukkhasamudayaṁ ariyasaccaṁ yathābhūtaṁ abhisamecca, dukkhanirodhaṁ ariyasaccaṁ yathābhūtaṁ abhisamecca, dukkhanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ ariyasaccaṁ yathābhūtaṁ abhisamecca sammā dukkhassantaṁ karissāmī’ti—
root_text.sn56.32:4.2: ‘ahaṁ padumapattānaṁ vā palāsapattānaṁ vā māluvapattānaṁ vā puṭaṁ karitvā udakaṁ vā tālapattaṁ vā āharissāmī’ti—
root_text.sn56.32:4.4: evameva kho, bhikkhave, yo evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.sn56.32:4.5: ‘ahaṁ dukkhaṁ ariyasaccaṁ yathābhūtaṁ abhisamecca …pe… dukkhanirodhagāminiṁ paṭipadaṁ ariyasaccaṁ yathābhūtaṁ abhisamecca sammā dukkhassantaṁ karissāmī’ti—
root_text.sn56.32:5.1: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yogo karaṇīyo …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yogo karaṇīyo”ti.

translation_text.sn56.32:1.2: ‘Without truly comprehending the noble truths of suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path, I will completely make an end of suffering.’
translation_text.sn56.32:2.1: It’s as if someone were to say:
translation_text.sn56.32:2.2: ‘I’ll make a basket out of acacia leaves or pine needles or myrobalan leaves, and use it to carry water or a palm frond.’
translation_text.sn56.32:2.4: In the same way, suppose someone were to say:
translation_text.sn56.32:2.5: ‘Without truly comprehending the noble truths of suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path, I will completely make an end of suffering.’
translation_text.sn56.32:3.1: But suppose someone were to say:
translation_text.sn56.32:3.2: ‘After truly comprehending the noble truths of suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path, I will completely make an end of suffering.’
translation_text.sn56.32:4.1: It’s as if someone were to say:
translation_text.sn56.32:4.2: ‘I’ll make a basket out of lotus leaves or flame-of-the-forest leaves or camel’s foot creeper leaves, and use it to carry water or a palm frond.’
translation_text.sn56.32:4.4: In the same way, suppose someone were to say:
translation_text.sn56.32:4.5: ‘After truly comprehending the noble truths of suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the path, I will completely make an end of suffering.’
translation_text.sn56.32:5.1: That’s why you should practice meditation …”

variant_text.sn56.32:2.2: saralapattānaṁ → salalapattānaṁ (bj); palāsapattānaṁ (cck, sya1ed, km, pts1ed); saralasapattānaṁ (sya2ed)
